title: sqlite: fix use-after-free in StatementSync due to premature GC
category: premature-GC use-after-free (statement holds a raw pointer to a collectible database)
crash_site: SIGSEGV / use-after-free in StatementSync::All() / Run() / Iterate() when they access db_ after the parent DatabaseSync was garbage-collected. Those methods are correct; they dereference a db_ pointer that was never kept alive.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the StatementSync methods where it crashes (All/Run/Iterate) — correct readers of db_; the DatabaseSync finalizer / sqlite3_finalize. The bug is the constructor storing a non-owning raw DatabaseSync*.
ground_truth.fix_summary: StatementSync's constructor stored its parent as a raw DatabaseSync* (db_ = db) with no strong reference, so V8 could garbage-collect/finalize the DatabaseSync while a StatementSync still pointed at it -> dangling db_ -> UAF on the next statement operation. Fix: change the member to BaseObjectPtr<DatabaseSync> db_ (constructor takes BaseObjectPtr, db_(std::move(db))), so the statement keeps the database alive; all uses become db_.get().

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: crash is in a statement method dereferencing db_, but the cause is the constructor holding a non-owning raw pointer to a GC-managed database. Live decoy = the crashing method. Authored from PR #56840 fix diff (raw DatabaseSync* -> BaseObjectPtr<DatabaseSync>).

provenance: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/56840 — sqlite: fix UAF in StatementSync due to premature GC (merged 2025-02-05); fix commit cebf4c8a9f23; base 316014d17273. Root cause + fix from the commit diff (StatementSync ctor raw ptr -> BaseObjectPtr<DatabaseSync>).
